title: Claude Just Made Marketing Agencies IRRELEVANT…

eval.summary: A shallow YouTube ad disguised as a tutorial. The video describes downloading community-contributed .md 'skill files' from a GitHub repo and installing them into Claude.ai's web interface via the 'Skills' customization panel. It then pivots to promoting a Chrome extension called MyPromptBuddy. There is no novel technique here — the 'skill' mechanism is just a system-prompt injection through Claude.ai's GUI. No API usage, no automation, no workflow design, no measurable token efficiency improvement, no exportable data, and no integration path to the Veritas stack. The stack already handles this via CLAUDE.md, .lux/ config dicts, and the VIE prompt construction pipeline — with full programmatic control. The Chrome extension sponsor is GUI-only prompt tooling with zero API surface. Nothing here passes P3 (no token efficiency data), P1 (GUI-only, no API path), or P7 (100% overlap with existing CLAUDE.md and VIE pipeline).
techniques_mentioned: system prompt injection via GUI skill files
eval.fit_rationale: GUI-only Claude.ai skill installation with no API surface duplicates what CLAUDE.md and VIE already do programmatically and with full stack control.

stack_evaluation.replaces_or_complements: nothing — fully redundant with CLAUDE.md working memory and VIE prompt construction pipeline
stack_evaluation.reasoning: Fails P1 (GUI-only, no API or export path for installed skills), fails P3 (zero token efficiency measurement or evidence), and fails P7 (CLAUDE.md + VIE pipeline already covers 100% of this domain with programmatic control). The MyPromptBuddy extension is a GUI Chrome tool with no integration surface and is also sponsor content, not a genuine recommendation.

title: SECRET Million Dollar App Idea in 60 Secs (AI Hack)

eval.summary: Short-form viral content teaching a basic app ideation loop: screenshot a popular App Store app, paste into Claude, ask for market gap analysis, then prompt Claude to suggest AI-augmenting features, then vibe-code the app. The 'hack' is using Claude's vision capability to analyze competitor app screenshots and reviews. No code, no architecture, no API, no measurable workflow. The '$15M high school students' claim is unverified clickbait. The only technically grounded element is using Claude multimodal input for competitive analysis — a technique already trivially available and well-documented. Nothing here advances the Veritas stack.
eval.fit_rationale: Content is consumer-grade app ideation hype with no integration path, no API surface, no workflow definition, and no measurable efficiency gain relevant to any Veritas vertical.

stack_evaluation.reasoning: Fails P3 — no token efficiency measurement or workflow baseline exists; this is a 60-second pitch, not an evaluated technique. Fails P9 — no declared revenue, capability, or family bucket; it is generic ideation content with no named client, KPI, or product. Fails P7 by implication — the only actionable element (Claude multimodal competitive analysis) is already executable today with existing Claude API calls and adds no new file or component worth evaluating.

title: This is the one tool that actually gets smarter every single time you use it. #ai #aitools #recall

eval.summary: Short-form promotional video for 'Recall', a Chrome extension that saves web content (YouTube, PDFs, podcasts, websites) into a personal knowledge base with AI-powered chat and retrieval. The pitch centers on a 'second brain' use case — save anything, query it later with timestamps. The tool supports multiple LLM backends including Claude, GPT, and Gemini. No technical depth, no API discussion, no export documentation, no pricing. Pure top-of-funnel affiliate/referral content with a comment-to-DM CTA.
eval.fit_rationale: Recall is a closed GUI Chrome extension with no documented API or export path, violating P1, and its knowledge-base function overlaps with VIE pipeline and Drive-based memory already in the stack (P7).

stack_evaluation.replaces_or_complements: Overlaps with VIE pipeline (nlm_feed_builder.py), AI Research Feed on Drive, and structured memory in .lux/data/
stack_evaluation.reasoning: Fails P1 immediately — Chrome extension GUI with no documented API or programmatic export path means zero integration with FastAPI/n8n orchestration layer. Fails P7 — VIE + Drive already handles save-summarize-retrieve over consumed content with full ownership. Fails P6 — multi-LLM backend (GPT, Gemini) contradicts Anthropic-only constraint, and no bearer-token auth model is described. Generic affiliate content with no technical substance to evaluate further.

title: How to build a Second Brain (prompt) #claude #claudecode #openclaw

eval.summary: A promotional video selling a $15 'second brain' prompt that instructs Claude Code to set up Obsidian as a session-log vault, with agent files pointing Claude back to that vault on startup, and claimed semantic file linking. The system is described as end-of-session logging + agent context injection — essentially a manual CLAUDE.md + structured notes pattern dressed up as a product. No API, no webhook, no export spec, no token efficiency data, no workflow declarations. The 'Andre Karpathy uses this' name-drop is unverifiable and likely misleading. Core mechanic (session logs written to a vault, read back on next session) is already implemented in the Veritas stack via CLAUDE.md, session-log, and .lux/data/*.json (P8). A $15 prompt cannot be ADOPT-worthy when P7 overlap with existing Working + Long-term memory surfaces is near 100%, and P3 has zero evidence of token reduction. This is a product sales video, not a technical contribution.
eval.fit_rationale: The core mechanic duplicates existing Veritas memory surfaces (CLAUDE.md, session-log, .lux/data/*.json) with no demonstrated token efficiency gain and no API-exportable data path.

stack_evaluation.replaces_or_complements: Directly overlaps Working Memory (CLAUDE.md, session-log) and Long-term Memory (GitHub, Drive) — already canonical stores per P8
stack_evaluation.reasoning: P7 fails immediately: overlap with existing Working + Long-term memory surfaces is ~100%, no BUILD_OR_EXTEND justification possible. P3 fails: zero token efficiency data provided, only marketing claims. P1 fails: Obsidian is a local GUI app; no documented API call exports state within one hour without GUI clicks unless the Obsidian Local REST plugin is separately configured, which is not mentioned.

title: 5 Secret Claude Hacks You NEED To Know

eval.summary: A 60-second YouTube short listing five alleged 'secret codes' for Claude: L99, /ghost, OODA, scaffold, and 'ultra think.' The video claims these prefixes trigger special Claude behaviors like deeper reasoning, human-sounding output, or structured planning. None of these are documented Claude API features, system prompt directives, or Anthropic-published capabilities. They are prompt priming tricks at best — informal instructions that may or may not produce consistent output. There is no API, no export path, no token efficiency measurement, no workflow integration, and no evidence of reproducible performance gains. The content is surface-level social media engagement bait ('comment send') dressed as technical insight. Zero actionable integration path for the Veritas stack.
eval.fit_rationale: No documented API behavior, no measurable token efficiency gain, and no integration surface — purely informal prompt tricks with no reproducible stack value.

stack_evaluation.reasoning: Fails P3 outright — no baseline, no eval rubric, no measured token reduction, just anecdotal claims. Fails P1 — no API, no export path, no documented behavior. Fails P7 — any prompt engineering work overlaps 100% with existing CLAUDE.md system prompt and VIE pipeline prompt templates, meaning no new file or integration is justified. These are informal natural-language prefixes with undefined and likely inconsistent effects on Claude's output.

title: Claude Code Just Killed $10,000 Websites (Here’s Proof)

eval.summary: A short-form YouTube video demonstrating a four-step workflow for building animated websites using Claude Code, Framer Motion, a UI kit called 'UIUX Pro Max,' and a component library from 21first.dev. The content is surface-level tutorial marketing — no architectural depth, no API/webhook patterns, no token efficiency data, no integration logic. The workflow is essentially: install Claude Code, install Framer Motion, drop in a UI skill, pull a component library. The output is a static/animated website, not an agentos pipeline component, not a data surface, not a workflow tool. There is zero relevance to Veritas stack concerns: no BD automation, no CRM integration, no orchestration pattern, no memory surface, no revenue workflow. Claude Code is already a known tool in the stack context; this video adds nothing to its operational use. The 'proof' claim in the title is a lead magnet for a PDF. No principles are meaningfully engaged — P3 (token efficiency) is unmeasured, P7 (overlap) flags this as fully covered by existing Claude Code usage, and P9 (revenue/capability/family bucket) cannot be tagged to any active Veritas workflow.
eval.fit_rationale: Frontend website generation via Claude Code has no connection to any active Veritas pipeline — not BD automation, not AgentOS, not TradeOS, not VIE.

stack_evaluation.reasoning: Fails P3: zero token efficiency data presented, no measurable task comparison. eval.summary: Short promotional video where Jake Van Clief explains that Claude Design (Anthropic's design-focused Claude product) consumes heavy tokens, then teases that he has replicated its functionality manually using a custom folder architecture of markdown files and skills — mirroring how Anthropic structures Claude Code skill files on GitHub. He claims dramatically lower token usage by reading skills at the right time rather than letting Claude Design automate the orchestration. No concrete implementation is shown; the video is a teaser pointing to his YouTube channel and a free school community. The core insight — that Claude Design's methodology is just structured markdown skill files in a folder hierarchy, which can be self-hosted to control token spend — is directionally relevant but entirely surface-level here. No code, no benchmarks, no reproducible steps.
eval.fit_rationale: The markdown-skill-file pattern for structuring Claude Code workflows is directly relevant to Alan OS and VIE pipeline architecture, but the video provides zero implementation detail.

stack_evaluation.replaces_or_complements: Complements VIE pipeline and Claude API usage patterns — potentially informs how CLAUDE.md and skill prompts are structured to reduce token burn
stack_evaluation.reasoning: The technique (folder-structured markdown skill files sequenced at task time) could pass P3 if the claimed token reduction is real and measurable, and it aligns with P8 named memory surfaces. However, the video is pure teaser with no benchmarks, no code, and no reproducible steps — P3's acceptance test (30% token reduction on 10 representative tasks) cannot be evaluated from this content. P7 also flags risk: CLAUDE.md and the existing VIE prompt architecture already touch this domain, so any extension needs overlap analysis before building. Re-evaluate if a concrete implementation video surfaces.

title: Clone any app with AI in 30 sec #ai #aiautomaionagency #aitools #chatgpt #aiagency #aichatbot #saas

eval.summary: A 60-second demo short showing a no-code workflow: screenshot a UI, paste into CopyCoder AI to generate a prompt, feed that prompt into Bolt.new, and deploy a shallow clone. The result ('MeTube') is a frontend shell with no backend functionality. This is surface-level AI hype content — the workflow produces a non-functional UI scaffold, not a production app. No API integrations, no data handling, no orchestration logic. The tools (Bolt.new, CopyCoder AI) are consumer-grade vibe-coding utilities. Neither integrates with the Veritas stack's Anthropic-only LLM constraint, FastAPI architecture, or n8n orchestration layer. There is zero token efficiency measurement, no ADR path, and no vertical config pattern. The content teaches nothing operationally useful for Alan OS, AgentOS, TradeOS, or any live Veritas vertical.

title: PROVEN AI Avatar System: Post 10x Daily for $2.83!

eval.summary: A short-form promotional video showcasing an AI avatar content system that generates and posts 10 pieces of content simultaneously across five social media platforms for $2.83 total. The presenter demonstrates a workflow using an AI avatar (looks/sounds like him), automated b-roll generation, and scheduled publishing. No technical depth is provided — no stack, no API names, no workflow architecture, no code. This is a lead-gen hook for a DM funnel, not actionable technical content. The claimed capability (avatar video + multi-platform posting at ~$0.28/video) is interesting as a cost benchmark but the video reveals nothing about the underlying toolchain.
eval.fit_rationale: No technical substance, no named tools, no API/integration detail — purely a lead-gen teaser with zero actionable signal for stack decisions.

stack_evaluation.reasoning: Fails P1 (no tools named, no API surface identified), fails P3 (no token efficiency data or workflow detail to evaluate), and fails P7 (no overlap analysis possible with zero technical disclosure). The $2.83 cost benchmark is mildly interesting but not worth a MONITOR slot — if avatar-based content distribution becomes relevant to Veritas client delivery, evaluate a purpose-built session on tools like HeyGen, Synthesia, or ElevenLabs directly.
